Parth Parmar
About Resume Blog

January 9, 2026 - Reliability, Serving

Improving inference systems without pausing delivery

Few teams can stop product work long enough to rebuild an inference stack. The more realistic approach is to improve the system at its highest-friction seams while traffic and feature delivery continue.

Model-backed services often accumulate fragile code around retries, fallback behavior, request shaping, or feature preparation. Those layers become risky when teams either avoid touching them or promise a full rewrite they cannot sustain.

A better method is to define a modernization policy. Improve observability first. Standardize how rollouts happen. Pull request traces and latency views closer to everyday engineering work. Then tighten one serving path at a time.

Use product changes as leverage

Whenever an inference-backed feature needs new behavior, use the change window to clean up adjacent system boundaries, remove special cases, and add minimal safety checks. Small upgrades compound.

Make failure modes visible

Teams can live with old systems longer than they think if the systems are observable and controllable. Hidden failure modes are usually more dangerous than legacy code by itself.

Parth Parmar.
